description.en: The Women In Science and Engineering-Societal Challenges with Opportunities (WISE-SCOPE) Fellowship program provides opportunities to women scientists and technologists who have completed Ph.D. and want to utilize their science & technology knowledge for the benefit of society.
description.hi: WISE-SCOPE फेलोशिप महिला वैज्ञानिकों और प्रौद्योगिकीविदों को अवसर प्रदान करती है जिन्होंने पीएच.डी. पूरी कर ली है और समाज के लाभ के लिए अपने विज्ञान और प्रौद्योगिकी ज्ञान का उपयोग करना चाहती हैं।
description.mr: WISE-SCOPE फेलोशिप महिला शास्त्रज्ञ आणि तंत्रज्ञांना संधी प्रदान करते ज्यांनी पीएच.डी. पूर्ण केली आहे आणि समाजाच्या फायद्यासाठी त्यांच्या विज्ञान आणि तंत्रज्ञान ज्ञानाचा वापर करू इच्छितात.
description.kn: WISE-SCOPE ಫೆಲೋಶಿಪ್ ಪಿಎಚ್.ಡಿ ಪೂರ್ಣಗೊಳಿಸಿದ ಮತ್ತು ಸಮಾಜದ ಪ್ರಯೋಜನಕ್ಕಾಗಿ ತಮ್ಮ ವಿಜ್ಞಾನ ಮತ್ತು ತಂತ್ರಜ್ಞಾನ ಜ್ಞಾನವನ್ನು ಬಳಸಿಕೊಳ್ಳಲು ಬಯಸುವ ಮಹಿಳಾ ವಿಜ್ಞಾನಿಗಳು ಮತ್ತು ತಂತ್ರಜ್ಞರಿಗೆ ಅವಕಾಶಗಳನ್ನು ಒದಗಿಸುತ್ತದೆ.

benefits.en: Monthly Fellowship of ₹55,000/- per month along with HRA as per norms. Selected fellows receive a research grant of ₹2.00 Lakhs per year, field demonstration support of ₹1,00,000/-, and equipment assistance up to a maximum of ₹2,00,000/-.
benefits.hi: नियमों के अनुसार एचआरए के साथ ₹55,000/- प्रति माह फेलोशिप। प्रति वर्ष ₹2.00 लाख का अनुसंधान अनुदान, ₹1,00,000/- की फील्ड प्रदर्शन सहायता और ₹2,00,000/- तक की उपकरण सहायता।
benefits.mr: नियमांनुसार एचआरए सह ₹५५,०००/- प्रति महिना फेलोशिप. दरवर्षी ₹२.०० लाख संशोधन अनुदान, ₹१,००,०००/- फील्ड प्रात्यक्षिक सहाय्य आणि ₹२,००,०००/- पर्यंत उपकरण सहाय्य.
benefits.kn: ನಿಯಮಗಳ ಪ್ರಕಾರ ಎಚ್‌ಆರ್‌ಎ ಜೊತೆಗೆ ತಿಂಗಳಿಗೆ ₹55,000/- ಫೆಲೋಶಿಪ್. ವರ್ಷಕ್ಕೆ ₹2.00 ಲಕ್ಷ ಸಂಶೋಧನಾ ಅನುದಾನ, ₹1,00,000/- ಫೀಲ್ಡ್ ಪ್ರಾತ್ಯಕ್ಷಿಕೆ ನೆರವು ಮತ್ತು ₹2,00,000/- ವರೆಗೆ ಉಪಕರಣ ಸಹಾಯ.

documentsRequired: Endorsement from the Head of the Institute

en: Step 1: Visit the official ePMS portal at https://onlinedst.gov.in.
Step 2: Click on 'New PI Registration' and register as 'WISE-SCOPE Fellow'.
Step 3: Log in and go to 'Submit proposals'.
Step 4: Enter General information, Mentor details, and Financial Details.
Step 5: Fill in PFMS details, upload required documents, and click 'Submit'.
Step 1: Visit the official ePMS portal at https://onlinedst.gov.in.
Step 2: Click on 'New PI Registration' and register as 'WISE-SCOPE Fellow'.
Step 3: Log in and go to 'Submit proposals'.
Step 4: Enter General information, Mentor details, and Financial Details.
Step 5: Fill in PFMS details, upload required documents, and click 'Submit'.

description.en: A Research Grant by MoST for female candidates to pursue research in frontier areas of science and engineering, preferably those having a break in career (minimum two years) and not having regular employment.
description.hi: MoST द्वारा महिला उम्मीदवारों के लिए विज्ञान और इंजीनियरिंग के क्षेत्र में अनुसंधान करने के लिए एक शोध अनुदान।

benefits.en: Provides monthly fellowship for 3 years: ₹55,000/- pm for Ph.D. or equivalent, ₹40,000/- pm for M.Phil./M.Tech, and ₹31,000/- pm for M.Sc. or equivalent.
benefits.hi: 3 वर्षों के लिए मासिक फेलोशिप प्रदान करता है: पीएच.डी. के लिए ₹55,000/- प्रति माह।

en: Step 1: Register at online portal https://online-wosa.gov.in/wosa/.
Step 2: Log in and fill the Project Proposal under WOS-A.
Step 3: Upload all required documents and click 'Final Submit'.
Step 1: Register at online portal https://online-wosa.gov.in/wosa/.
Step 2: Log in and fill the Project Proposal under WOS-A.
Step 3: Upload all required documents and click 'Final Submit'.

description.en: Women Scientist Scheme-B (WOS-B) focuses on projects related to Science & Technology (S&T) interventions for societal benefit.

benefits.en: Provides monthly fellowship for 3 years: ₹55,000/- pm for Ph.D. or equivalent, ₹40,000/- pm for M.Phil./M.Tech, and ₹31,000/- pm for M.Sc. or equivalent.

en: Step 1: Visit the official department website to get the proposal format.
Step 2: Fill out the application form under WOS-B.
Step 3: Attach the project proposal and submit online.
Step 1: Visit the official department website to get the proposal format.
Step 2: Fill out the application form under WOS-B.
Step 3: Attach the project proposal and submit online.

description.en: A flagship program of the KIRAN Division of the Department of Science & Technology (DST) offering one year of on-the-job training in the area of Intellectual Property Rights (IPR).

benefits.en: Provides one year of training with a monthly stipend: ₹20,000/- pm for M.Sc./B.Tech/MBBS, ₹25,000/- pm for M.Tech/M.Pharma/M.VSc, and ₹30,000/- pm for Ph.D.

en: Step 1: Check notifications on www.pfc.org.in when open.
Step 2: Register online and enter required credentials.
Step 3: Fill qualifications and upload certificates, then submit.
Step 1: Check notifications on www.pfc.org.in when open.
Step 2: Register online and enter required credentials.
Step 3: Fill qualifications and upload certificates, then submit.

description.en: Launched by the Ministry of Women and Child Development (MWCD) to tap the talent of women students, scholars, social activists, and teachers from non-tier-I cities and rural areas.

benefits.en: Monthly stipend of ₹20,000/- per month, travel reimbursement, hostel facility in Delhi, triple sharing room, and certification.

en: Step 1: Visit the official MWCD internship portal.
Step 2: Click on 'Register' and enter details.
Step 3: Log in and upload your photo.
Step 4: Fill the application and submit.
Step 1: Visit the official MWCD internship portal.
Step 2: Click on 'Register' and enter details.
Step 3: Log in and upload your photo.
Step 4: Fill the application and submit.

description.en: A scheme by AICTE under the Ministry of Education to provide financial assistance and support to girl students pursuing technical diploma studies in India.

benefits.en: ₹50,000/- per annum for every year of study (max 3 years) towards payment of college fees, purchase of computer, books, equipment, and software.

en: Step 1: Visit the National Scholarships Portal (NSP).
Step 2: Register on the portal, accept terms and click continue.
Step 3: Log in using your Application ID and password sent via SMS.
Step 4: Fill out the application form, upload scanned copies of documents and submit.
Step 1: Visit the National Scholarships Portal (NSP).
Step 2: Register on the portal, accept terms and click continue.
Step 3: Log in using your Application ID and password sent via SMS.
Step 4: Fill out the application form, upload scanned copies of documents and submit.

description.en: CBSE Udaan Scheme empowers girl students in Classes 11 and 12 aiming for STEM careers, providing them free coaching and preparatory classes for entrance exams like IIT-JEE and NEET.

benefits.en: Free online and offline learning support, weekly virtual contact classes in science/math, free textbooks, study guides, mentoring sessions, and tablets.

en: Step 1: Visit the official CBSE Udaan Scheme website.
Step 2: Register on the portal by providing name, school, and qualifications.
Step 3: Fill out the application form and upload scanned documents.
Step 4: Submit the application and await school verification.
Step 1: Visit the official CBSE Udaan Scheme website.
Step 2: Register on the portal by providing name, school, and qualifications.
Step 3: Fill out the application form and upload scanned documents.
Step 4: Submit the application and await school verification.

description.en: A national initiative to provide assured, comprehensive, and quality antenatal care (ANC) free of cost to all pregnant women on the 9th of every month.

benefits.en: Free comprehensive health checkups, diagnostics, ultrasound scans, and clinical treatments conducted by medical officers.

en: Step 1: Walk in to the nearest government healthcare center, PHC, CHC, or hospital.
Step 2: Attend the special clinics set up on the 9th of any month.
Step 3: Register at the desk and receive free checkups.
Step 1: Walk in to the nearest government healthcare center, PHC, CHC, or hospital.
Step 2: Attend the special clinics set up on the 9th of any month.
Step 3: Register at the desk and receive free checkups.

description.en: A 100% State Sponsored Medical Assistance scheme by the Department of Labour, Jharkhand. Provides financial assistance to registered female construction workers for their deliveries.

benefits.en: Financial assistance of ₹15,000/- for the first two deliveries to the registered female construction worker.

documentsRequired: Proof of Registration with JBOCWW Board

en: Step 1: Visit Jharkhand labour portal.
Step 2: Log in using your credentials.
Step 3: Go to Services > BOC Scheme Benefit > Application Form.
Step 4: Fill details, upload documents, and submit.
Step 1: Visit Jharkhand labour portal.
Step 2: Log in using your credentials.
Step 3: Go to Services > BOC Scheme Benefit > Application Form.
Step 4: Fill details, upload documents, and submit.

description.en: Financial Assistance implemented by the Directorate of Women and Child Development, Govt. of Goa, to improve the Female Child Sex Ratio in the State.

benefits.en: Provides ₹10,000/- as a one-time financial incentive directly credited to the mother's bank account upon delivering a live girl child.

en: Step 1: Obtain the form from Anganwadi Centre or ICDS Block Office.
Step 2: Fill the form with birth details and bank info.
Step 3: Submit to CDPO within 45 days of delivery.
Step 1: Obtain the form from Anganwadi Centre or ICDS Block Office.
Step 2: Fill the form with birth details and bank info.
Step 3: Submit to CDPO within 45 days of delivery.

description.en: A scheme by the Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas to provide clean cooking fuel (LPG connections) to women from poor households, improving their health and environment.

benefits.en: Provides free LPG connection, financial assistance of ₹1,600 per connection, and interest-free loan for stove and first refill.

en: Step 1: Visit pmuy.gov.in or nearest LPG distributor.
Step 2: Submit the KYC application form along with Aadhaar and Ration Card.
Step 3: Once verified, LPG connection is issued under the woman's name.
Step 1: Visit pmuy.gov.in or nearest LPG distributor.
Step 2: Submit the KYC application form along with Aadhaar and Ration Card.
Step 3: Once verified, LPG connection is issued under the woman's name.

description.en: National Mission for Financial Inclusion to ensure access to financial services, namely, basic savings accounts, remittance, credit, insurance, and pension in an affordable manner.

benefits.en: Interest on savings deposit, Rupay Debit card with ₹2 Lakh accident insurance cover, zero balance account, and overdraft facility up to ₹10,000 to eligible account holders.

en: Step 1: Visit the nearest commercial bank branch or Bank Mitra outlet.
Step 2: Fill out the PMJDY account opening form.
Step 3: Submit the form along with Aadhaar copy and photo.
Step 1: Visit the nearest commercial bank branch or Bank Mitra outlet.
Step 2: Fill out the PMJDY account opening form.
Step 3: Submit the form along with Aadhaar copy and photo.

description.en: Provides partial wage compensation and nutrition incentives to pregnant women and lactating mothers for safe motherhood and child healthcare.

benefits.en: Cash incentive of ₹5,000 in two instalments for the first child, and ₹6,000 for the second child (if it's a girl child).

en: Step 1: Register at your local Anganwadi Centre (AWC) or health facility.
Step 2: Submit Form 1-A with documents within 150 days of LMP.
Step 3: Submit Form 1-B and child birth proof for subsequent benefits.
Step 1: Register at your local Anganwadi Centre (AWC) or health facility.
Step 2: Submit Form 1-A with documents within 150 days of LMP.
Step 3: Submit Form 1-B and child birth proof for subsequent benefits.

description.en: Provides food, shelter, clothing, legal aid, clinical aid, and training to women in difficult circumstances who are in need of institutional support for rehabilitation.

benefits.en: Free temporary residential accommodation, food, medical care, legal aid, counselling, and vocational training.

documentsRequired: Police FIR/Reference from social welfare (if applicable)

en: Step 1: Walk in to a registered Swadhar Greh or shelter home.
Step 2: Implementing agency verifies distress and arranges admission.
Step 3: Health checkup and counselling are conducted within three days.
Step 1: Walk in to a registered Swadhar Greh or shelter home.
Step 2: Implementing agency verifies distress and arranges admission.
Step 3: Health checkup and counselling are conducted within three days.

description.en: Sanctioned by the Govt. of Karnataka to assist women in gaining self-reliance through self-employment, mainly in trade and service sectors, by providing loan subsidies through banks.

benefits.en: For SC/ST candidates, up to 50% subsidy on loan unit costs between ₹1,00,000 to ₹3,00,000. For general/special, 30% subsidy (up to ₹90,000). Includes EDP training.

en: Step 1: Visit nearest bank or KSFC branch with DPR.
Step 2: Complete the Udyogini business loan application.
Step 3: Bank processes loan and requests corporation for subsidy.
Step 1: Visit nearest bank or KSFC branch with DPR.
Step 2: Complete the Udyogini business loan application.
Step 3: Bank processes loan and requests corporation for subsidy.

description.en: Implemented by Ministry of Social Justice. Aims to provide women entrepreneurs from backward classes (OBCs) term loans for self-employment.

benefits.en: Provides term loan of up to ₹2,00,000/- at a highly subsidized interest rate of 5% per annum. No self-contribution is required.

en: Step 1: Visit nearest State Channelising Agency (SCA) office.
Step 2: Fill out Swarnima Scheme application form.
Step 3: Submit application with OBC certificate and income proof.
Step 1: Visit nearest State Channelising Agency (SCA) office.
Step 2: Fill out Swarnima Scheme application form.
Step 3: Submit application with OBC certificate and income proof.

description.en: An aggregator platform initiated by NITI Aayog that hosts information and services relevant to women entrepreneurs, enabling mentorship and funding access.

benefits.en: Access to workshops, mentoring, startup support courses, community forums, and partner funding resources.

en: Step 1: Register online at https://wep.gov.in/.
Step 2: Create profile describing your entrepreneurship domain.
Step 3: Browse and apply for partner mentoring/funding programs.
Step 1: Register online at https://wep.gov.in/.
Step 2: Create profile describing your entrepreneurship domain.
Step 3: Browse and apply for partner mentoring/funding programs.

description.en: A scheme by the Ministry of Finance to facilitate bank loans between ₹10 Lakh and ₹1 Crore to SC/ST and/or women entrepreneurs for greenfield projects.

benefits.en: Composite bank loans from ₹10 Lakhs up to ₹1 Crore. Sidbi portal hand-holding support and refinancing options.

en: Step 1: Visit standupmitra.in or any bank branch.
Step 2: Complete the registration describing the greenfield business.
Step 3: Provide required financial/DPR reports and submit application.
Step 1: Visit standupmitra.in or any bank branch.
Step 2: Complete the registration describing the greenfield business.
Step 3: Provide required financial/DPR reports and submit application.

description.en: A self-employment scheme in the coir industry providing training in coir spinning and motorized ratts to rural women artisans in coir-producing coastal regions.

benefits.en: Free training, ₹3,00,0 stipend per month during training, subsidized coir ratts and equipment, and PMEGP loan linkage.

en: Step 1: Check notifications from local Coir Board training centers.
Step 2: Apply for the coir spinning training programme.
Step 3: Complete training and submit application for rattle subsidy.
Step 1: Check notifications from local Coir Board training centers.
Step 2: Apply for the coir spinning training programme.
Step 3: Complete training and submit application for rattle subsidy.

description.en: Provides collateral-free loans up to ₹10 Lakhs for micro and small enterprises in manufacturing, trading, and services.

benefits.en: Collateral-free loans in three categories: Shishu (up to ₹50,000), Kishor (up to ₹5 Lakhs), and Tarun (up to ₹10 Lakhs).

en: Step 1: Apply online through the Udyami Mitra portal (udyamimitra.in) or visit any commercial bank.
Step 2: Choose loan category (Shishu, Kishor, Tarun).
Step 3: Submit application with KYC documents and project report.
Step 1: Apply online through the Udyami Mitra portal (udyamimitra.in) or visit any commercial bank.
Step 2: Choose loan category (Shishu, Kishor, Tarun).
Step 3: Submit application with KYC documents and project report.

title.en: Support to Training and Employment Programme for Women (STEP)
title.hi: महिलाओं के लिए प्रशिक्षण और रोजगार सहायता कार्यक्रम (STEP)

description.en: Under the Ministry of Women and Child Development, it aims to provide skill training and employment linkages to poor and asset-less women in traditional sectors like agriculture, dairying, and handlooms.

benefits.en: Free vocational skill training, access to credit, support services (child care and healthcare), and forward/backward integration.

en: Step 1: Locate an NGO or cooperative implementing STEP projects in your area.
Step 2: Submit admission form at the training agency.
Step 3: Complete the training and receive certificate/employment assistance.
Step 1: Locate an NGO or cooperative implementing STEP projects in your area.
Step 2: Submit admission form at the training agency.
Step 3: Complete the training and receive certificate/employment assistance.

description.en: Managed by the Centre for Cultural Resources and Training (CCRT) to identify and support young talented children in artistic fields (music, dance, drama, etc.) by providing specialized training and funding.

benefits.en: Scholarship amount of ₹3,600 per year, and reimbursement of tuition fee up to ₹9,000 per year for specialized training.

en: Step 1: Register on the National Scholarships Portal (NSP) or CCRT portal.
Step 2: Fill out the application and submit portfolio/receipt of training.
Step 3: Selection is done via screening and audition/interview.
Step 1: Register on the National Scholarships Portal (NSP) or CCRT portal.
Step 2: Fill out the application and submit portfolio/receipt of training.
Step 3: Selection is done via screening and audition/interview.

description.en: A credit-linked subsidy programme by Ministry of MSME to generate self-employment opportunities through setting up new micro-enterprises in non-farm sectors.

benefits.en: Bank loan with government margin money subsidy ranging from 15% to 35% (up to 35% margin money subsidy for special categories like women, SC/ST, rural areas). Project cost up to ₹50 Lakhs for manufacturing.

documentsRequired: Educational Certificate (VIII Pass minimum for >10 Lakh project)

en: Step 1: Visit the PMEGP e-Portal run by KVIC.
Step 2: Fill online application and upload project report.
Step 3: Application is forwarded to bank for loan sanctioning and KVIC for subsidy.

amount: Up to 35% subsidy on ₹50 Lakh project

en: Step 1: Visit the PMEGP e-Portal run by KVIC.
Step 2: Fill online application and upload project report.
Step 3: Application is forwarded to bank for loan sanctioning and KVIC for subsidy.

amountLocale.en: Up to 35% subsidy on ₹50 Lakh project

description.en: Provides financial assistance to startups for Proof of Concept, prototype development, product trials, market entry, and commercialization.

benefits.en: Grants up to ₹20 Lakhs for proof of concept, and up to ₹50 Lakhs seed funding through debt or convertible debentures.

en: Step 1: Go to seedfund.startupindia.gov.in.
Step 2: Submit details of your recognized startup and upload pitch deck.
Step 3: Select preferred incubators for evaluation and funding approval.
Step 1: Go to seedfund.startupindia.gov.in.
Step 2: Submit details of your recognized startup and upload pitch deck.
Step 3: Select preferred incubators for evaluation and funding approval.

title.en: Indira Gandhi National Widow Pension Scheme (IGNWPS)
title.hi: इंदिरा गांधी राष्ट्रीय विधवा पेंशन योजना

description.en: Centrally sponsored scheme by Ministry of Rural Development to provide pension security to widows from BPL families.

benefits.en: Monthly pension of ₹300/- per month for widows aged 40 to 79. The amount increases to ₹500/- per month from age 80 onwards.

en: Step 1: Visit web.umang.gov.in/web_new/home or open UMANG App.
Step 2: Search for NSAP and click 'Apply Online'.
Step 3: Enter details, upload photo and submit.
Step 1: Visit web.umang.gov.in/web_new/home or open UMANG App.
Step 2: Search for NSAP and click 'Apply Online'.
Step 3: Enter details, upload photo and submit.

description.en: Implemented by Government of Himachal Pradesh to provide livelihood pension and economic support to poor widows, abandoned women, and unmarried women.

benefits.en: Monthly pension of ₹1,150/- disbursed directly to the bank account.

en: Step 1: Obtain application form from Gram Panchayat or block development office.
Step 2: Fill out form and attach required certificates.
Step 3: Submit completed form to Block Development Office.
Step 1: Obtain application form from Gram Panchayat or block development office.
Step 2: Fill out form and attach required certificates.
Step 3: Submit completed form to Block Development Office.

title.hi: मणिपुर गरीब विधवा वित्तीय सहायता योजना

description.en: A scheme by Department of Social Welfare, Manipur, providing social security to widows in the age group of 18-40 years who are not covered under IGNWPS.

benefits.en: Financial assistance at the rate of ₹2,000/- per annum per widow, subject to fund availability.

en: Step 1: Download application form from socialwelfare.nic.in or collect from CDPO/DSWO.
Step 2: Fill details and attach self-attested documents.
Step 3: Submit the form to the District Social Welfare Officer concerned.
Step 1: Download application form from socialwelfare.nic.in or collect from CDPO/DSWO.
Step 2: Fill details and attach self-attested documents.
Step 3: Submit the form to the District Social Welfare Officer concerned.

description.en: Digital Complaint Registration and referral system for women affected by violence or in distress across India, run from the premises of the National Commission for Women, New Delhi.

benefits.en: 24/7 telephonic/digital counselling, psychological support, and referrals to police, hospitals, and crisis centers.

en: Step 1: Go to ncwwomenhelpline.ncw.gov.in or dial the short-code 181.
Step 2: Describe the distress issue and provide contact/location details.
Step 3: Counsellors arrange police or medical assistance instantly.
Step 1: Go to ncwwomenhelpline.ncw.gov.in or dial the short-code 181.
Step 2: Describe the distress issue and provide contact/location details.
Step 3: Counsellors arrange police or medical assistance instantly.

title.en: Relief and Rehabilitation of Acid Victims (Haryana)
title.hi: हरियाणा एसिड हमला पीड़िता राहत योजना

description.en: Launched by WCD Department, Government of Haryana, to provide financial and medical assistance to girl/women acid attack victims.

benefits.en: 100% free medical treatment at approved hospitals. Ad-hoc relief of ₹1,00,000/- (₹25,000 immediately, ₹75,000 within 15 days).

en: Step 1: Visit official Antyodaya-SARAL Portal.
Step 2: Log in using your registered credentials.
Step 3: Search for 'Relief and Rehabilitation of Women Acid Victims' in services list.
Step 4: Fill form details, upload documents, and submit.
Step 1: Visit official Antyodaya-SARAL Portal.
Step 2: Log in using your registered credentials.
Step 3: Search for 'Relief and Rehabilitation of Women Acid Victims' in services list.
Step 4: Fill form details, upload documents, and submit.

title.en: Financial Assistance to Acid Victims (Pension - Haryana)
title.hi: हरियाणा एसिड हमला पीड़िता पेंशन योजना

description.en: Social security pension scheme by Social Justice & Empowerment Department, Govt. of Haryana, providing a monthly pension based on disability percentage.

benefits.en: Monthly pension based on disability percentage: 2.5 times Divyang Pension (40-50% disability); 3.5 times (51-60% disability); 4.5 times (>61% disability).

documentsRequired: Copy of application made to WCD

en: Step 1: Visit Office of the District Social Welfare Officer (DSWO).
Step 2: Fill out application form and attach self-attested documents.
Step 3: Submit to DSWO and receive acknowledgment receipt.
Step 1: Visit Office of the District Social Welfare Officer (DSWO).
Step 2: Fill out application form and attach self-attested documents.
Step 3: Submit to DSWO and receive acknowledgment receipt.

description.en: An integrated women empowerment umbrella scheme by Ministry of WCD containing Sambal (safety) and Samarthya (empowerment) verticals, covering One Stop Centres, WHL, and PMMVY.

benefits.en: Welfare services, emergency helpline response, legal/medical aid, and maternity cash incentives up to ₹6,000.

en: Step 1: Contact national helpline 181 or visit nearest One Stop Centre.
Step 2: Submit details of requirement/maternity case.
Step 3: Access shelter, clinical aid, or DBT cash benefits directly.
Step 1: Contact national helpline 181 or visit nearest One Stop Centre.
Step 2: Submit details of requirement/maternity case.
Step 3: Access shelter, clinical aid, or DBT cash benefits directly.

description.en: Provides safe, secure, and conveniently located hostel accommodation for working women, along with daycare facilities for their children where possible.

benefits.en: Affordable rent (capped at 15% of salary for single room), stay up to 3-5 years, and daycare center access for children.

en: Step 1: Locate an approved Working Women Hostel in your city.
Step 2: Submit admission application form to the hostel manager.
Step 3: Provide employment certificate and salary certificate for rent calculations.
Step 1: Locate an approved Working Women Hostel in your city.
Step 2: Submit admission application form to the hostel manager.
Step 3: Provide employment certificate and salary certificate for rent calculations.

description.en: Livelihood and sports support initiative for women athletes and coaches in India, providing financial impetus and grassroot sports leagues.

benefits.en: Financial support, specialized coaching, training resources, and sports equipment support for elite women athletes.

en: Step 1: Visit the portal of Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports.
Step 2: Submit details of your sports accomplishments and achievements.
Step 3: Apply for training support or elite athlete allowance.
Step 1: Visit the portal of Ministry of Youth Affairs and Sports.
Step 2: Submit details of your sports accomplishments and achievements.
Step 3: Apply for training support or elite athlete allowance.

title.hi: खेलो इंडिया राष्ट्रीय खेल विकास कार्यक्रम

description.en: A national programme to promote sports culture, establish KISCE training centers, and identify young talent to provide scholarships.

benefits.en: Scholarship and funding of ₹6.28 Lakh per annum per athlete (including ₹10,000/month out-of-pocket allowance) and training in accredited academies.

en: Step 1: Register on the Khelo India Portal.
Step 2: Upload sports achievements and create profile.
Step 3: Shortlisted athletes undergo fitness and talent search trials.
Step 1: Register on the Khelo India Portal.
Step 2: Upload sports achievements and create profile.
Step 3: Shortlisted athletes undergo fitness and talent search trials.

description.en: Achieving Sports Milestone by Inspiring Women Through Action (ASMITA) Leagues promotes sports participation and competitive exposure among Indian women athletes.

benefits.en: Access to national women's sports leagues, specialized training, and business loan support via SBI Asmita.

documentsRequired: Sports achievement details / Business registration documents

en: Step 1: Visit the official MyGov campaign link or Stand-Up India/SBI portal.
Step 2: Fill application form and upload sports credentials.
Step 3: Apply for financial or coaching support.
Step 1: Visit the official MyGov campaign link or Stand-Up India/SBI portal.
Step 2: Fill application form and upload sports credentials.
Step 3: Apply for financial or coaching support.

description.en: Organizes rural poor women into Self Help Groups (SHGs) and provides revolving and capitalization funds to access financial services and sustainable livelihoods.

benefits.en: Capacity building, revolving funds (up to ₹30,000), Community Investment Funds (up to ₹2.5 Lakh), bank linkage, and interest subventions.

en: Step 1: Register on the Aajeevika NRLM portal.
Step 2: Create a Self Help Group profile with bank account details.
Step 3: Access revolving funds and capitalization support directly.
Step 1: Register on the Aajeevika NRLM portal.
Step 2: Create a Self Help Group profile with bank account details.
Step 3: Access revolving funds and capitalization support directly.

description.en: Small informal groups of 10 to 20 poor women who save regularly and lend internally to meet emergency needs, supported by NABARD.

benefits.en: Access to bank accounts, interest-earning savings, internal credit, and leadership capacity building.

en: Step 1: Contact local animator or NGO to form a group of 10-20 women.
Step 2: Set up savings accounts at the nearest commercial or cooperative bank.
Step 3: Begin regular savings and meetings.
Step 1: Contact local animator or NGO to form a group of 10-20 women.
Step 2: Set up savings accounts at the nearest commercial or cooperative bank.
Step 3: Begin regular savings and meetings.

description.en: A sub-component of DAY-NRLM to empower women in agriculture by making systematic investments to enhance their participation and sustainable livelihoods.

benefits.en: Skill training in sustainable agriculture, distribution of gender-friendly tools, capacity building, and market linkages.

en: Step 1: Register through your local SHG federation.
Step 2: Submit details of your agricultural activities.
Step 3: Access training and tools distribution programs.
Step 1: Register through your local SHG federation.
Step 2: Submit details of your agricultural activities.
Step 3: Access training and tools distribution programs.

description.en: Promotes menstrual hygiene among rural girls and women in Maharashtra by providing subsidized sanitary napkins through Self Help Groups.

benefits.en: Subsidized sanitary napkins distributed to school-going girls (₹5 pack) and rural women (subsidized cost), and hygiene awareness.

en: Step 1: SHGs register on the Asmita portal.
Step 2: Girls/women can buy subsidized packs from local SHGs or Anganwadis.
Step 1: SHGs register on the Asmita portal.
Step 2: Girls/women can buy subsidized packs from local SHGs or Anganwadis.

description.en: An initiative under DAY-NRLM to enable women in Self Help Groups (SHGs) to earn a sustainable annual household income above ₹1 Lakh through micro-enterprise support.

benefits.en: Capitalization support up to ₹20 Lakh per SHG, revolving funds, CIF support, low-interest bank loans, and marketing facilities (Saras centers).

en: Step 1: Join a local Self Help Group associated with NRLM.
Step 2: Engage in income-generating activities and apply at block level.
Step 3: Obtain marketing or micro-loan capitalization support.
Step 1: Join a local Self Help Group associated with NRLM.
Step 2: Engage in income-generating activities and apply at block level.
Step 3: Obtain marketing or micro-loan capitalization support.
